name: Great-grandma's Lemon Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the butter in a mixing bowl until smooth.
2. Gradually add sugar, beating well after each addition.
3. In a separate small bowl, beat egg yolks until thick and lemon-colored.
4. Add the yolk mixture to the creamed butter and sugar, mixing well.
5. Stir in the lemon peel and juice.
6. Combine flour and baking soda; add alternately with milk, stirring gently after each addition.
7. Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form.
8. Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the batter.
9. Pour the batter evenly into two greased 9x5x3 inch loaf pans.
10. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 65-70 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
11. Cool the cakes on wire racks for about 10 minutes.
12. Remove from pans and allow to cool completely before dusting with confectioner's sugar.
Yield: 2 cakes (24 servings)
If cooking for two, wrap one cake and freeze for later enjoyment.
A "Golden Oldie" recipe from Glenda Stokes, Florence, SC. This is one of my favorites copied from my mother's old cookbook. Reminisce J/A '93
